never used of magic will have to see how this can work for lfortran thought you were messing with me lol. I am using the plplot fortran libraries to create a "native" (yes it calls to c) plotting procedure for quickly plotting data. I was trying to compile the procedure and run it in lfortran but I can't seem to get it to import the necessary mod and compiled libraries to be picked up by the compiler. 

As for the config I am referring to is the kernel.json file that jupyter reads in it is located at `~/miniforge3/envs/lf/share/jupyter/kernels/fortran/kernel.json`

this edit would look something like this
```JSON
{
    "display_name": "Fortran",
    "argv": [
        "/home/blaine/miniforge3/envs/lf/bin/lfortran",
        "-I", 
        "/usr/local/lib/fortran/modules/plplot/",
        "-l",
        "plplotfortran",
        "kernel",
        "-f",
        "{connection_file}"
    ],
    "language": "fortran"
}
```
or this

```JSON
{
    "display_name": "Fortran",
    "argv": [
        "/home/blaine/miniforge3/envs/lf/bin/lfortran",
        "-I /usr/local/lib/fortran/modules/plplot/",
        "-l plplotfortran",
        "kernel",
        "-f",
        "{connection_file}"
    ],
    "language": "fortran"
}

```
However while I can have this option compile the module in lfortran as it does not complain that it can't find the plplot library (can't get it to compile the main program still working on that) it just refuses to import the library in kernel mode.

Here is my proof on concept project that has all the working code:
https://github.com/ChunkyPanda03/FortranPlot
and here is the command needed to compile it without xmake:
gfortran --verbose  -I /usr/local/lib/fortran/modules/plplot/ -l plplotfortran plotting.f90 test.f90 -l plplotfortran

you will just need this library to compile the program here is the deb for it: https://packages.debian.org/testing/libs/libplplotfortran0

**Key part of this:**
trying to compile it in lfortran by swapping out gfortran results in an internal compiler error however **it does not complain about not not finding the required library however it does while in the kernel mode.** I will open another issue about it not compiling however it is not the focus of this issue.

@certik I hope that this clears up any confusion.
